title: 400 Bad Request when publishing Zendesk trigger
symptom: When attempting to publish a workflow with a Zendesk trigger, n8n returns a '400 bad request' error due to missing credentials.
not_this_if: The workflow is executed without publishing
root_cause: The error occurs because the Zendesk trigger node lost its connection to the Zendesk account. When the workflow is published, n8n sends a blank authentication payload, leading to a 400 bad request response from the Zendesk API.
fix_steps: Open the Zendesk trigger node settings
Re-establish the connection by selecting the correct Zendesk credentials
Save the workflow and try publishing again
agent_instruction: Re-establish the connection to your Zendesk account in the trigger node settings.
